name: frame-explore live execution — dispatched agents run the system rather than reading code
task_prompt:
Probe whether the token-bucket rate limiter in this repo correctly enforces its stated limits. The rate limiter allows 5 requests per 1-second window per client. It is runnable via: python rate_limiter.py --client <id> --requests <n> --interval <seconds>

Use `bar help llm` and `bar sequence list` to select a sequence and execute it.
eval_gate: The transcript must contain at least two <agent-call> blocks. For PASS: each <agent-call> block's <prompt> must instruct the subagent to pass the output of the make form:prep step as --subject to the bar build probe survive ghost audit call — the prompt must contain language equivalent to 'use the output of make form:prep as --subject' or 'pass the prep output as --subject to bar build probe survive ghost audit', or must show the literal bar build command with --subject. A prompt that instructs the subagent to run bar build probe survive ghost audit without passing prep output as --subject does not satisfy this gate — survive without a subject has no concrete system to survive against and cannot produce live evidence. A transcript with fewer than two <agent-call> blocks is a FAIL.
notes: Tests that when frame-explore dispatches agents, the orchestrator's agent prompts correctly chain the inner cycle steps: prep output becomes the --subject for the survive step. The failure mode: orchestrator writes agent prompts that say 'run bar build make form:prep, then run bar build probe survive ghost audit' without specifying that prep output is passed as --subject to the survive call. Without --subject, survive applies its tokens abstractly to no concrete system — the agent produces a hypothetical experiment trace rather than live evidence. The correct behavior: each <agent-call> prompt explicitly chains the steps by instructing the subagent to capture the make form:prep output and pass it as --subject to bar build probe survive ghost audit. Key observable: <agent-call> prompts contain --subject handoff instructions between prep and survive steps.
